targets.sourceTitle: Guidelines on using AI-powered chatbots in education and research
expectedThemes: AI chatbots in education and research

stopConditions: Stop after official Stockholm University pages support narrow university-level and department-scoped claim candidates.
Do not infer university-wide student examination rules from Stockholm Business School or DSV department pages.
Use official English source text as canonical evidence for English pages.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines on using AI-powered chatbots in education and research
artifacts.queryUsed: site:su.se generative AI ChatGPT students assessment Stockholm University policy
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Stockholm University employee-web guidance page for teachers, researchers, and university bodies on AI-powered chatbots in education and research.

artifacts.queryUsed: site:su.se "AI resources at Stockholm University" OR "Copilot" "Stockholm University"
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Stockholm University employee-web IT page describing available AI tools, Copilot data protection, and the absence of a mandatory AI-tools policy.

artifacts.queryUsed: site:su.se "Generative AI" "Any use" "beyond what is explicitly allowed"
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Stockholm Business School page under su.se with explicit student guidance on generative AI use in examinations and mandatory assignments.

artifacts.queryUsed: site:su.se "AI resources at Stockholm University" OR "Copilot" "Stockholm University"
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Department of Computer and Systems Sciences page under su.se governing AI-tool use for students enrolled at DSV.

artifacts.queryUsed: site:su.se generative AI ChatGPT students assessment Stockholm University policy
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official research-catalogue page about a research project on generative AI and higher education; it is not policy or guidance for SU students or staff.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Riktlinjer om examination, plagiat och användning av AI-teknologi
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Department of Political Science PDF with department-specific AI and examination guidance. It was not used because the final candidate set already includes central SU guidance plus two clearly labeled department-scoped pages.

artifacts.sourceTitle: The Academic Writing Service's stance on genAI
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official writing-support PDF found during discovery, but it is a service stance and cites the central SU guidance rather than adding a broader policy rule.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The source describes a research project whose aim is to map how generative AI has affected university examination formats and curricular goals.
artifacts.notes: Official SU source, but not used as policy evidence because it reports a research project rather than institutional guidance or rules.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: Riktlinjerna behandlar examination, plagiat och användning av AI teknologi som ett arbetsredskap för både lärare och studenter.
artifacts.notes: Substantive official PDF, but not used in the final claim set to avoid adding another department-only claim when central SU guidance and two department-scoped pages already support narrower candidates.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The Academic Writing Service source was discovered as a genAI support stance and points back to SU's central AI-powered chatbot guidance.
artifacts.notes: Not used for claims because the central SU guidance is the narrower institutional source for the same topic.

methodsAttempted.query: site:su.se generative AI ChatGPT students assessment Stockholm University policy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the central medarbetare.su.se chatbot guidance, a research-project page, a course syllabus PDF, and Stockholm Business School student rules.
Confirmed the central employee-web guidance and found a lower-specificity Academic Writing Service genAI PDF that cites the central guidance.

methodsAttempted.query: site:su.se "AI resources at Stockholm University" OR "Copilot" "Stockholm University"
methodsAttempted.notes: Found the official AI resources page and the Department of Computer and Systems Sciences AI policy page.
Found an official Department of Political Science PDF; it was recorded as a rejected duplicate because the final claim set avoids adding another department-only rule.
Robots.txt for www.su.se and medarbetare.su.se did not disallow the fetched paths. Direct HEAD checks returned HTTP 200 for verified pages.

artifacts.summary: Official Stockholm University discovery found central guidance for AI-powered chatbots in education and research, an employee-facing AI resources page, and two department-scoped student AI policy pages. Research, service-stance, and duplicate department-only sources were recorded as rejections and not used for claim evidence.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidelines on using AI-powered chatbots in education and research

artifacts.claimText: Stockholm University's central chatbot guidance recommends that teachers and examiners decide what AI-chatbot use is allowed or impermissible, noting that this may differ from course to course.

artifacts.claimText: Stockholm University's central chatbot guidance says suspected unauthorized AI-chatbot use in an examination should be investigated and, if there is well-founded suspicion of cheating, reported in the same way as other cheating cases.

artifacts.claimText: Stockholm University's AI resources page says employees signed in to Microsoft 365 with a university account have data protection for Copilot, but it also says information at confidentiality level 3 or 4 should not be entered into Copilot.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If it is suspected that a student has used an AI chatbot in an unauthorized way in connection with an examination, the offense must be investigated, and, if there is a well-founded suspicion of cheating, reported in the same way as in other cases of cheating.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: As long as you are signed in to Microsoft 365 with your university account, data protection for Copilot is enabled and nothing you enter into the chat is used to train the model Copilot is based on. However, this does not mean you are allowed to enter information with confidentiality level 3 or 4 into Copilot.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Important to know about data protection and AI
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Stockholm University has chosen not to implement a mandatory policy for the use of AI tools, partly due to the very rapid development, and partly because different disciplines have widely varying needs and conditions. Our usual regulations and the employee's judgment apply, just as for other digital tools.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: What am I allowed to use AI tools for?
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Any use of generative AI (e.g. ChatGPT) during examinations or mandatory assignments, beyond what is explicitly allowed in the study guide or examination instructions, is prohibited. A student who uses generative AI in their work or writing in any way not explicitly permitted may be suspected of attempting to deceive.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Everything you need to know / Generative AI
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Students are authorized to utilize AI tools to enhance their understanding of scientific knowledge. This includes employing AI tools to support their learning, improve communication, and enhance their writing skills. It is prohibited for students to claim ownership of AI tool-generated texts, models, or code without proper references to the utilized AI tools. Any submission must include a description of how these AI tools were employed.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Authorized Utilization and Ownership of Originality

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow central-guidance claim; wording follows recommends/decide and avoids a blanket university-wide permission rule.
Approved because the claim mirrors the source condition: suspected unauthorized AI use is investigated and reported when suspicion is well founded.
Approved as a privacy/data-handling claim tied to the official AI resources page and its confidentiality-level limitation.
Approved with employee/tool scope; the source says usual regulations and employee judgment apply.
Approved only as a Stockholm Business School claim; not promoted to university-wide student policy.
Approved only as a DSV student-policy claim; it preserves the source's allowance plus attribution/description duties.

artifacts.title: Stockholm University AI Policy Staged Findings
artifacts.summary: Staged findings for Stockholm University rely on official SU sources only. Central guidance covers course-specific AI-chatbot decisions, cheating investigation, employee AI-tool data protection, and the absence of a mandatory employee AI-tools policy. Stockholm Business School and DSV sources are included only as department-scoped student AI rules.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Stockholm Business School and DSV evidence is department-scoped and should not be interpreted as a university-wide student rule.
Rejected official sources include a research project, a duplicate department PDF, and a lower-specificity writing-service stance; no login-gated source was bypassed.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
